intro: Imerinorchis nutans, also known as the Madagascar orchid, is a plant species belonging to the Orchidaceae family. The Madagascar orchid has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
